# Pikos

> Notes, tasks, and calendar in one local-first desktop app.

Pikos is a desktop productivity app for macOS that combines notes, tasks, and a calendar into a single interface. All data is stored locally in SQLite on the user's device. No account required, no sign-up, no cloud dependency.

## Key Facts

- Category: Productivity / Notes / Task Management / Calendar
- Platform: macOS (Tauri 2 + React)
- Pricing: Free download. Paid option on Mac App Store includes iCloud sync.
- Data: Local-first. SQLite database on device. Export as SQLite or JSON.
- Privacy: No telemetry, no tracking cookies, no personal data collection.
- Website: https://pikos.app
- Contact: hello@pikos.app

## What Pikos Does

- Rich text editor with slash commands, formatting toolbar, Markdown paste, and autosave
- Task management with priorities (Urgent/High/Medium/Low), statuses, tags, and folders
- Week calendar view with drag-to-schedule, timed and all-day events
- Quick add with natural language parsing (extracts dates, priorities, tags, folders)
- Full-text search with BM25 ranking
- Three-panel layout: sidebar, page list, editor or calendar
- Complete keyboard navigation
- Light, dark, and system themes
- Data export (SQLite backup, JSON)

## Pages

- Homepage: https://pikos.app
- Blog: https://pikos.app/blog
- Release Notes: https://pikos.app/release-notes
- Privacy Policy: https://pikos.app/privacy
- Terms of Service: https://pikos.app/terms

## Competitors and Alternatives

Pikos competes with NotePlan, Obsidian, TickTick, Things, Todoist, and Linear. It differentiates by combining notes, tasks, and calendar in a single local-first app with no account requirement.
